commands.review_summary:
Adds a public channel-broker SDK subpath, bundled channel-broker plugin, docs, tests, package/export wiring, labeler metadata, and Vitest shard registration.

PR surface: Source +1123, Tests +230, Docs +202, Config +75, Generated 0, Other +14. Total +1644 across 31 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes for the review findings from source inspection: the diff claims overlapping prefixes, sends raw token strings, and drops the existing send `AbortSignal`; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide the public broker API and bundled-vs-external ownership before an automated repair lane should modify this draft.

target.title: [codex] Add channel broker plugin protocol
target.body:
## Summary

This PR lands the Phase 1 foundation for consolidating chat channel maintenance behind a provider-agnostic Channel Broker surface.

- Adds a public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` contract with versioned broker request, receipt, capability, health, and target helpers.
- Adds a bundled `channel-broker` extension that adapts provider-owned messaging mechanics onto OpenClaw's existing channel message lifecycle.
- Keeps OpenClaw responsible for message/session semantics: routing, durable final sends, previews, receipts, retries, `/verbose`, and auditability.
- Intentionally does not migrate Telegram, Discord, or Slack yet; those are tracked as Phase 2 follow-ups.
- Success for this PR is a reviewed, discoverable, documented broker foundation with focused tests and real behavior proof.

## Linked context

Closes #86107

Related #86105
Related #86108
Related #86109
Related #86110
Related #86111
Related #86112
Related #86113
Related #86114
Related #86115

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? Yes: maintainer request to reduce native channel maintenance burden by moving platform mechanics behind extension/provider-owned broker contracts.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw can discover the new bundled `channel-broker` channel from a real checkout and the new SDK helpers produce the provider-facing target/outbound protocol shape.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, Node `v25.8.2`, PR head `0f81a445d0`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));'`
- Evidence after fix (terminal output from the real checkout):

```json
{
  "discoveredChannel": {
    "id": "channel-broker",
    "order": 60,
    "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker"
  },
  "channelNormalization": "channel-broker",
  "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7",
  "outboundProtocolVersion": 1,
  "outboundMode": "final",
  "outboundPlatform": "telegram",
  "outboundConversation": {
    "id": "chat 123",
    "type": "channel",
    "threadId": "topic-7"
  },
  "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real bundled channel catalog discovers `channel-broker`, channel id lookup resolves it, the Telegram-style broker target string preserves topic/thread scope, and the outbound request normalizes to protocol version `1` with platform `telegram` for provider delivery.
- What was not tested: End-to-end delivery through a third-party broker provider and migration of native Telegram/Discord/Slack adapters; those are Phase 2 follow-up work.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is Phase 1 protocol/plugin proof, not a live third-party-provider delivery proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): No exact `channel-broker` bundled channel or SDK subpath existed before this branch.

## Tests and validation

Local validation from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`:

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts`
- `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`

Known broader gates that are red on clean current `upstream/main` before this PR diff:

- `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` fails existing SDK surface budgets (`public entrypoints 315 > 303`, `public exports 9094 > 8449`, etc.; with this PR it becomes one public entrypoint larger).
- `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` fails existing unrelated changed-doc label entries. This PR adds the Channel Broker glossary entries it introduces.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No` for defaults; this adds a new bundled broker surface.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes`; new `channel-broker` config/docs/API surface exists, but native channel defaults are unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`; the broker plugin defines signed/provider-owned HTTP delivery surfaces and outbound provider calls. This PR only introduces the foundation and tests; live provider migrations come later.

What is the highest-risk area? Accidentally letting platform mechanics leak back into core or treating constrained platforms as if they had full official bot/API parity.

How is that risk mitigated? Broker capability types, opaque `channelData`, extension boundary checks, focused conformance tests, and issue-first follow-up PRs for each migration phase.

## Current review state

Next action: wait for ClawSweeper final review on #86096, then address any actionable feedback. If there are no actionable blockers, mark the PR ready for maintainer review.

Still waiting on: ClawSweeper final review and maintainer decision. Current `gh pr checks` has no pending/failing current checks; prior failed/cancelled Real behavior proof runs were superseded after this PR body gained structured proof.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: Real behavior proof gate was addressed by adding copied terminal output from the real checkout. No inline review threads or human reviews are currently posted.
